A.label: Balanced Highlights
A.desc: A balanced plan that covers the strongest relevant picks for the user's stated interests with a steady full-day pace.
A.badge: Best for a broad first pass through the destination

B.label: Alternative Mix
B.desc: A more exploratory plan that avoids repeating the first option where possible and surfaces different but still relevant alternatives.
B.badge: Best for travelers who want a different take on the same trip brief

C.label: Focused Depth
C.desc: A coherence-first alternative that keeps the trip compact, protects stronger thematic fit, and trades a little breadth for a more internally consistent route.
C.badge: Best for travelers who want a more coherent version of the same brief

A.seed_reason: This cluster pairs traditional cultural sites (Buddhist temple and history museum) with multiple zen-style garden experiences (Imperial Palace East National Gardens, Hamarikyu Gardens, Kiyosumi Gardens, Japanese Garden) for a coherent, fine-dining-friendly day pacing.
Kiyosumi Gardens, teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM (garden, art museum)

Hamarikyu Gardens pairs classic Edo-era style landscaping with a tight cluster of art museums (National Art Center, Mitsubishi Ichigokan, National Museum of Modern Art, and teamLab Borderless), creating a culturally rich traditional-meets-contemporary day well-suited to a zen-garden and dining-friendly pace without repeating the already-used zen-gardens theme.

B.seed_reason: Zojo-ji Temple, Japanese Garden (buddhist temple, garden)
Koishikawa Korakuen, Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Ginza Six Rooftop Garden (garden)
The National Art Center, Tokyo, Mitsubishi Ichigokan Museum, Sensō-ji Denboin Garden (art museum, garden)

C.seed_reason: This cluster of major museums—traditional cultural exhibitions (Tokyo National Museum) alongside Western and modern art museums—creates a coherent, low-friction cultural day that still supports fine browsing and classic dining options nearby.
